result:
The build passes with no warnings. The task is complete.

I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, alongside the existing `NeedsQuoting`:

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is parsed to its closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside kept as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure guarantees the edge cases — the empty line parses to one empty field, and a trailing comma produces a final empty field — so `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips every non-empty list.

Everything is pure computation with no I/O or global state, and `NeedsQuoting` is unchanged.
